edge_hypothesis: Every EMA crossover test to date used 1.0x ATR stops — the same stop width that caused DD blowouts in supertrend (181%) and rsi_pullback (185%). The advisory concludes 'wide R:R works only with high-quality signals' but the EMA crossover wide-exit test (PF=0.891) only widened TP, NOT stops. The crossover signal itself is lagging by design — EMA_9/21 crosses AFTER the move begins, meaning the initial bar often includes a retracement that stops out 1.0x positions. By widening stops to 1.5-2.5x ATR, we give the lagging signal room to prove correct through the initial noise. If trending-regime PF stays at ~1.27 with wider stops, the overall PF should improve because fewer valid trending signals get stopped out prematurely. This is a stop-width experiment, not a signal-quality experiment. Regime assumption: TRENDING (EMA crosses are trend-following signals). Invalidation: if PF < 0.95 with wider stops, the EMA crossover signal truly lacks edge — stop width was not the binding constraint.

variants.name: ema_cross_wide_stop_moderate_tp
variants.description: EMA_9/21 crossover with 2.0x ATR stop (double the standard 1.0x) and 3.0x ATR TP. The wider stop gives the lagging crossover signal room to develop through initial retracement noise. 1.5:1 R:R is modest but prioritizes not getting stopped out. Tests the hypothesis that stop width, not signal quality, is the binding constraint for EMA crossover.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Data required: ETH OHLCV on 4h and 1h
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (3.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: wider stop increases max loss per trade, may produce larger DD if crossover signals are truly random
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 3.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: ema_crossover confirmation — crossover itself is the signal
ema_crossover volatility gate — 2.0x ATR wide stop experiment

variants.name: ema_cross_widest_stop_runner
variants.description: Maximum stop width experiment: 2.5x ATR stop with 4.0x ATR TP. This is the most patient variant — it gives the crossover signal the absolute maximum room to develop. The 1.6:1 R:R is still positive-expectancy if win rate exceeds 38%. Tests the outer boundary of how much stop-width helps a low-quality signal. If this variant produces PF > 1.0 while the standard 1.0x stop variant doesn't, stop-width is confirmed as the critical variable.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Data required: ETH OHLCV on 4h and 1h
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (4.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: very wide stops mean each losing trade is expensive — need high win rate to compensate
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 4.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: ema_crossover volatility gate — 2.5x ATR widest stop

variants.name: ema_cross_safe_floor
variants.description: Conservative stop-width variant: 1.5x ATR stop (the advisory-recommended minimum floor) with 3.5x ATR TP. This is the mildest departure from standard — tests whether even a small stop increase (1.0x to 1.5x) meaningfully improves PF. The 2.3:1 R:R is closer to the fee-killer ratio that worked for MACD. If this outperforms standard (PF~0.98), the stop-floor recommendation is validated specifically for EMA crossover.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Data required: ETH OHLCV on 4h and 1h
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (3.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: 1.5x stop may still be too tight for the EMA crossover lag if the cross typically occurs 2+ ATR into the move
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 3.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: ema_crossover volatility gate — 1.5x ATR stop floor
